name: Etsy Product Photography: Rules, Specs and Best Practice

step.name: Pick your window and clear the space
step.text: Choose a room with a large north-facing window. Clear a table near the window and turn off every indoor light to avoid mixed colour temperatures.

step.name: Set up your infinity sweep backdrop
step.text: Tape a sheet of white poster board to the table and curve it up the wall behind to create a seamless background with no visible horizon line.

step.name: Position your reflector
step.text: Place a white foam board or reflector opposite the window within two feet of the product to bounce light back into the shadow side.

step.name: Mount your camera on a tripod
step.text: Set phone or camera on a mini tripod at product height. Frame the product in the middle 70 percent of the shot. Use the rear camera in portrait mode.

step.name: Shoot the full sequence for each product
step.text: Shoot at minimum: hero, scale reference, detail shot, and alternate angle. Use a self-timer and aim for 5 to 10 frames per product.

step.name: Process to Etsy specs in batch
step.text: Crop each to 3000 x 3000 pixels, apply consistent brightness and white balance, and export as JPG at 80-90 percent quality under 1 MB per file.

step.name: Upload and test at thumbnail size
step.text: Upload to Etsy with hero shot as photo 1. Use the thumbnail adjustment tool and check how it reads at 25 percent browser zoom.
